reply.proposal: Replace the ad-hoc middleware chain in packages/api with Fastify's encapsulated plugin system to enforce explicit, testable ordering via lifecycle hooks. Extract auth, rate limiting, and request logging into isolated plugins that use onRequest/preHandler hooks and share state via request decoration. Replace hand-rolled logic with maintained libraries: @fastify/jwt with jose for auth verification, @fastify/rate-limit backed by Redis for distributed rate limiting, and pino-http for structured request logging. Centralize validation and error handling using @fastify/type-provider-zod with a global error handler to ensure consistent 401/429/500 responses. Add contract tests asserting middleware execution order and failure isolation before incrementally migrating existing routes.

Install @reduxjs/toolkit and react-redux in apps/dashboard and create a centralized store at src/lib/store.ts using configureStore with typed RootState and AppDispatch exports. Define feature slices (e.g., authSlice, uiSlice, dataSlice) in src/lib/features/ using createSlice to encapsulate reducers and actions, replacing scattered useState and prop-drilling. Wrap the dashboard root layout / App entry with react-redux Provider and create typed useAppSelector and useAppDispatch hooks for type-safe usage throughout components. Migrate existing messy global state incrementally to Redux selectors and dispatches, leveraging Redux DevTools for debugging and leaving room to adopt RTK Query later for data-fetching.

Install prisma as a dev dependency and @prisma/client as a dependency in packages/api and run npx prisma init to scaffold prisma/schema.prisma and .env. Configure the datasource to use postgresql (or your current DB) with env("DATABASE_URL") and define initial models by translating existing hand-written SQL tables or by introspecting with prisma db pull, plus set generator client. Create a singleton PrismaClient instance in packages/api/src/lib/prisma.ts with globalThis caching for dev hot-reload and export it for all services/routes. Replace raw SQL string queries throughout the API with type-safe prisma.model.findMany/create/update calls and add package.json scripts for prisma generate, prisma migrate dev, and prisma studio. Verify with a build and a simple query test and document env setup in README.

Create a dedicated BillingClient module in packages/api/src/clients/billing that wraps all billing provider interactions behind a typed interface. Use Node.js native fetch (powered by undici) to avoid extra heavy dependencies, combined with AbortSignal.timeout for per-request timeouts and AbortController for overall deadlines. Implement resilient retries using p-retry with exponential backoff and jitter, retrying only on safe cases like network errors, 429 and 5xx, and skipping 4xx. Validate all responses with zod schemas and throw a custom BillingProviderError that includes status code, request ID, and sanitized body, integrated with our existing pino logger and Sentry for full observability.

Do not make the session token readable from JavaScript; the long-lived session must remain in an HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Lax (or Strict) cookie that is never accessible via document.cookie. The SPA should not manually manage the token but let the browser attach it automatically by using fetch with credentials: 'include' and same-origin or properly CORS-configured API. Enforce CSRF protection via SameSite combined with anti-CSRF tokens / double-submit checks for state-changing requests. If the downstream API requires an Authorization header, introduce a Backend-for-Frontend (BFF) on the same origin that reads the HttpOnly cookie server-side and proxies the request with the header. If a short-lived access token must be used in JS, keep it only in an ephemeral in-memory variable (never persisted) and renew it via silent refresh using the HttpOnly refresh token.
acknowledged_rejections: Using non-HttpOnly cookies readable via document.cookie
Exposing session tokens on window object or persisted JS state
Manually attaching session tokens from JavaScript to fetch Authorization header

reply.proposal: Install jest, ts-jest, and @types/jest as devDependencies directly in packages/api via the workspace package manager to keep test tooling isolated to the API package. Create a jest.config.ts at packages/api root using preset ts-jest, testEnvironment node, roots pointing to src, moduleNameMapper aligned with tsconfig paths, and setup to clear mocks between tests. Add test and test:watch scripts to packages/api/package.json and add a __tests__ or *.test.ts colocation pattern for handler unit tests with mocked dependencies. This gives us isolated, fast unit tests for request handlers without affecting other packages and prepares us for future coverage thresholds in CI.

We will use CSS Modules colocated with each marketing component in apps/dashboard, e.g., Hero/Hero.tsx with Hero/Hero.module.css alongside it, leveraging Next.js built-in CSS Modules support. Global design tokens for colors, spacing, and typography will be defined as CSS Variables in apps/dashboard/styles/globals.css and consumed within the modules. Each component will import its local styles object and apply classes via styles.*, ensuring styles are fully scoped and live next to the component as the designer prefers. This avoids runtime overhead while keeping maintenance and design handoff simple.

Make `src/db/schema.ts` (Drizzle ORM) the single source of truth for the DB schema. Use Drizzle Kit to diff the current TypeScript schema against the `drizzle/` folder to auto-generate versioned SQL migration files. Add npm scripts `db:generate` and `db:migrate` and run migrate on deploy, with a CI check that fails if schema changes are present without a committed migration. Allow manual edits inside generated SQL files only as an escape hatch for data migrations.

Introduce a GraphQL gateway facade in front of our existing REST services. We define a strongly-typed schema for all public resources and let mobile clients declare exactly the fields they need via selection sets, eliminating overfetching. Resolvers delegate to current service layer and push field projections down to SQL/Mongo to only fetch requested columns. We add DataLoader for batching/N+1 prevention, persisted queries plus max depth/complexity limits for performance and security, while keeping REST running for backward compatibility.

cells.context:
Project conventions and prior decisions:
- Keep the API server on Hono — Hono's middleware model covers auth, logging and rate limiting with minimal overhead, and it runs identically on Node and edge runtimes
- Use Zustand for client state in the dashboard — Our global state surface is small (session, filters, toasts); Zustand covers it in ~50 lines without ceremony
- Query Postgres with the postgres.js driver and hand-written SQL — Direct SQL keeps query plans visible and avoids a runtime abstraction layer; typed row mappers live next to each query
- Use native fetch with a small retry wrapper for outbound HTTP — Node 18+ ships fetch; one 40-line wrapper gives retries, timeouts and JSON handling without a dependency
- Store session tokens in httpOnly SameSite=Lax cookies — Keeps tokens out of reach of injected scripts; the browser attaches them automatically so the SPA never touches the raw token
- Run unit tests with the built-in node:test runner — Zero test-framework dependencies; node --test runs TypeScript output directly and CI needs no transform config
- Style the dashboard with Tailwind utility classes — No runtime style computation, works with React Server Components, and design tokens live in one config
- Cache session lookups in a Postgres UNLOGGED table — Keeps the stack at exactly one datastore; UNLOGGED tables benchmarked within 15% of Redis for our session read pattern
- Hand-written, numbered, forward-only SQL migrations — Every schema change is reviewed as literal SQL; forward-only keeps prod and CI history linear
- Public API stays REST with an OpenAPI spec — HTTP caching, straightforward auth, and every client language has mature tooling; sparse fieldsets cover the overfetching complaints
- Structured logging with pino, one JSON line per request — Fast, structured, and the ops dashboards parse JSON lines natively
- CI runs on GitHub Actions with a single reusable workflow — One workflow file, matrix per package; no CI vendor lock beyond what the repo host already implies
- pnpm workspace for the monorepo — Strict node_modules layout catches phantom dependencies; workspace protocol keeps internal packages linked
- Token-bucket rate limiting at the API edge, per API key — Predictable burst behavior; limits configured per plan tier
- All API errors use the problem+json envelope — RFC 7807 gives clients one error shape; internal codes map to documented types
- Local development runs through docker compose — Postgres and the API start with one command; parity with the deploy image
- Prettier with the shared config; ESLint flat config for lint rules — Formatting is not reviewed; lint rules are; keeping the tools separate keeps both fast
- Feature flags read from environment variables, no flag service — A dozen flags do not justify a flag platform; env vars deploy atomically with the code
- OpenTelemetry traces exported to the collector, sampled at 10% — Vendor-neutral instrumentation; sampling keeps ingest costs flat
- User file uploads go to S3-compatible object storage, never the database — Keeps the database small and backups fast; presigned URLs offload transfer
- Zod schemas validate every API input at the boundary — One schema per endpoint doubles as the OpenAPI source of truth
- API is versioned in the URL path (/v1), breaking changes bump the version — Explicit and cache-friendly; header versioning confused two integration partners in the spike
- Each package keeps a README with run and test instructions — Onboarding doc lives next to the code it describes
- UI strings go through the i18n catalog from day one — Retrofitting i18n was a six-week project at the last company; catalog-first is cheap now

reply.proposal: Keep Hono and consolidate packages/api around a single createApp() factory that registers middleware in a canonical, tested order: request-id -> OpenTelemetry (10% sampled) -> pino request logger -> token-bucket rate limiter -> auth (httpOnly SameSite=Lax cookie + API key) -> Zod validation. Refactor the existing hand-rolled auth, rate limiting, and logging logic into typed Hono MiddlewareHandler factories that reuse our current implementations: pino for JSON-line logging, Postgres UNLOGGED table with postgres.js for session and rate-limit counters, and problem+json for all error responses. Enforce ordering with a linter rule and node:test tests that assert the chain, and document the chain in the package README.
acknowledged_rejections: Migrating API server from Hono to Express/Fastify/Koa for better middleware ergonomics
Introducing Redis for rate limiting and session cache
Switching public API from REST/OpenAPI to GraphQL/tRPC to simplify middleware

reply.proposal: The messiness is from a single growing store, not from Zustand itself. Split apps/dashboard state into small domain-scoped Zustand stores (session, filters, toasts, etc.) co-located with their features, each exposing typed selectors and actions. Enforce a consistent slice pattern and add zustand's devtools + immer middleware for debugging and immutable updates, documented in the dashboard README. This keeps our global state surface minimal and within the ~50-line model we chose, without the bundle size and boilerplate of Redux.
acknowledged_rejections: Redux Toolkit for apps/dashboard global state

reply.proposal: Reject setting up Prisma in packages/api to respect the prior decision to use postgres.js with hand-written SQL for visible query plans and zero ORM overhead. Keep the existing postgres.js driver and forward-only numbered SQL migrations as the source of truth for schema. Achieve the requested type safety by co-locating strict TypeScript interfaces and Zod-validated row mappers next to each query module and leveraging postgres.js typed sql tag. For complex queries, add shared Row type helpers and a lint rule that disallows unmapped `any` rows, which preserves reviewable SQL while giving compile-time safety. Update the package README to document this pattern instead of adding Prisma Client.

reply.proposal: Reuse the existing outbound HTTP pattern in packages/api — native Node 18+ fetch wrapped in our small 40-line retry helper — to build a dedicated billing client module rather than introducing a new dependency. The module will configure base URL and auth from env vars, use AbortSignal.timeout for per-request deadlines, retry idempotent calls on 429 and 5xx with exponential backoff + jitter, and validate all responses through Zod schemas for the billing contract. Errors are logged as structured JSON via pino with attempt count, status and trace ids, wrapped in an OpenTelemetry span, and mapped to our standard problem+json error types for consistent reporting to callers. Cover retry/timeout/error branches with node:test and document env vars and usage in the package README.
key_technologies: existing fetch retry wrapper in packages/api

reply.proposal: Keep session tokens in httpOnly SameSite=Lax cookies and do not expose the raw token to JavaScript. The browser attaches the cookie automatically, so the mobile SPA does not need to read or manually attach it - fetch calls should use credentials: 'include' to send the cookie. This preserves the existing XSS mitigation where injected scripts cannot exfiltrate the token, while still supporting authenticated fetch. If the SPA needs explicit session state, expose a /v1/session or /v1/me endpoint that returns non-sensitive metadata rather than the token itself.
acknowledged_rejections: Storing session tokens in localStorage or sessionStorage for JavaScript access
Using non-httpOnly cookies readable via document.cookie
Exposing session token to JavaScript to attach manually to Authorization header

reply.proposal: Do not add Jest to packages/api; we will honor the prior decision to use the built-in node:test runner to keep zero test-framework dependencies and no transform config in CI. For request handler unit tests we will colocate *.test.ts files with handlers and exercise them via Hono's app.request() helper, asserting responses with node:assert/strict. Mock Postgres and outbound fetch with node:test's built-in mock.fn/module and a thin stub for postgres.js to keep query mappers testable. Update packages/api package.json test script to node --test and document it in the package README, leaving the existing GitHub Actions reusable workflow untouched.
acknowledged_rejections: Adding Jest to packages/api as the test runner

reply.proposal: Keep the public /v1 REST API and add standardized sparse fieldsets via `?fields=id,name,avatar` (or `?fields[resource]=...` for compound docs) to let mobile clients request only what they need. Validate the `fields` param at the Hono boundary with Zod against a per-resource allowlist sourced from the OpenAPI spec, returning a problem+json error for unknown fields. Push the validated projection into the hand-written postgres.js SQL as a safe dynamic SELECT list so we only read/serialize requested columns, defaulting to the full resource when the param is absent for backwards compatibility. Document the param in the OpenAPI spec and include it in the cache key with appropriate Vary handling, preserving existing pino logging and rate limiting.
acknowledged_rejections: GraphQL endpoint / BFF GraphQL layer for client-driven field selection - prior decision keeps Public API as REST with OpenAPI spec; sparse fieldsets were explicitly chosen to cover overfetching

Project decision log:
- Keep the API server on Hono — Hono's middleware model covers auth, logging and rate limiting with minimal overhead, and it runs identically on Node and edge runtimes
  - REJECTED: Express — Larger dependency surface and slower cold starts; the middleware ecosystem is tempting but everything we need exists for Hono. Migration was evaluated and declined 2026-03

  - REJECTED: Prisma — Query engine adds cold-start latency on serverless deploys and hides generated SQL; we hit both in the 2026-02 spike
  - REJECTED: TypeORM — Decorator-based entities fought our functional codebase style

reply.proposal: Refactor packages/api to use Hono's native middleware composition instead of hand-rolled wrappers, keeping the server on Hono. Create typed middleware factories in src/middleware/ for request-id/opentelemetry, pino request logging, token-bucket rate limiting (backed by the existing Postgres UNLOGGED table), and auth (reading httpOnly SameSite=Lax cookies), and compose them with app.use() in a single explicit order. Each middleware stays covered by node:test unit tests and Zod remains at the route boundary, with the ordering and rationale documented in packages/api README.

reply.proposal: Reuse Node 18+ native fetch with our existing 40-line retry wrapper for the billing provider client in packages/api, keeping outbound HTTP to one audited implementation. Configure timeouts via AbortController with exponential backoff + jitter for 429 and 5xx, and map provider failures into our problem+json error envelope with good messages. Validate all requests and responses at the boundary with Zod schemas and emit one structured JSON line per call via pino with OpenTelemetry trace propagation. This gives us retries, timeouts, and error reporting with zero additional dependencies, consistent with the rest of the stack.

reply.proposal: Keep the public API as REST on Hono at /v1 and solve overfetching with sparse fieldsets, not a new query language. Add an optional `fields` query param (JSON:API style `fields[resource]=id,name`) validated at the boundary by Zod against a per-resource allowlist that is documented in the OpenAPI spec. The postgres.js layer projects only the requested columns in hand-written SQL SELECTs, reducing payload and DB I/O, and invalid fields return a problem+json error. This is additive within v1, preserves HTTP caching (cache key includes fields / Vary), and requires no new datastore or runtime.

  - REJECTED: localStorage — XSS exfiltration — the 2026-04 pentest demonstrated token theft through a compromised analytics snippet reading localStorage
  - REJECTED: sessionStorage — Same XSS exposure as localStorage, plus tokens lost on every new tab
- Token-bucket rate limiting at the API edge, per API key — Predictable burst behavior; limits configured per plan tier

- Hand-written, numbered, forward-only SQL migrations — Every schema change is reviewed as literal SQL; forward-only keeps prod and CI history linear

  - REJECTED: GraphQL — Resolver N+1s and cache invalidation complexity outweigh flexibility for our ~30-endpoint surface; evaluated 2026-05 with the mobile team present

- Store session tokens in httpOnly SameSite=Lax cookies — Keeps tokens out of reach of injected scripts; the browser attaches them automatically so the SPA never touches the raw token
  - REJECTED: localStorage — XSS exfiltration — the 2026-04 pentest demonstrated token theft through a compromised analytics snippet reading localStorage
  - REJECTED: sessionStorage — Same XSS exposure as localStorage, plus tokens lost on every new tab

We will keep the current practice of hand-written, numbered, forward-only SQL migrations where every change is reviewed as literal SQL. This practice exists because a generated diff silently included a column drop that reached staging in 2026-03 and had to be rolled back, so generated DDL is no longer trusted. To reduce the pain of writing SQL by hand, we can add editor snippets, a SQL lint step, and a CI job that diffs expected schema vs migration history to catch drift, without executing generated DDL.

reply.proposal: Retain the REST API with /v1 URL versioning and solve overfetching with sparse fieldsets as previously decided, e.g. GET /v1/users?fields=id,displayName,avatarUrl or JSON:API-style GET /v1/articles?fields[articles]=title,summary&fields[author]=name. Implement a shared 'fields' query-param middleware that validates requested fields against an allowlist derived from OpenAPI component schemas and returns 400 for unknown fields. Pass the allowed projection to the service/ORM layer to SELECT only needed columns and prune the serializer output, keeping responses HTTP-cacheable. Document the fields param in the OpenAPI spec as a comma-separated enum per resource and ship client helpers to drive adoption on mobile.
